ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-10-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Epstein for and with Julius Lichtenstein (Petitioner). Franchesca Callejo for Esmeralda Nolasco. Gene Sverdlov for Maria Corriea.

Mr. Epstein addressed the Attorney's fees. Mr. Lichtenstein expressed his concerns with regard to property maintenance, rents, taxes, liens, etc. Mr. Epstein advised the Court that his Client wants to pursue rent charges prior to the rental agreement which was signed in June of 2009. Ms. Callejo reported that her Client was doing well, that she is being cared for by the Daughter, the Niece and the Son in Law, spoke of the condition of the house, the Corriea's income, etc. The Court stated that it is not inclined to re-visit the issue of rents, or the issue of distribution and credits. The Court approved the accounting petition (subject to review of the order). The Petition for Instructions was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-05-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Epstein for and with Julius Lichtenstein (Petitioner). Franchesca Callejo (Guardian Ad Litem for Esmerelda Nolasco, life tenant). Gene Sverdlov fir Maria Corriea.

Ms. Callejo reported on Mrs. Nolasco's current situation. Mr. Epstein stated the Petition for Instructions to Charge Maria Corriea with attorney's fees is withdrawn. He filed the Second Accounting this morning and hearing is set on May 9, 2012. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to having/not having sufficient funds to keep the trust property. Petition for Instruction re Eviction of Beneficiary was continued to May 09, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Off Calendar Aug-22-2011 Continued To Sep-12-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Epstein for and with Julius Lichenstein (Petitioner/Trustee). Gene Sverdlov for Maria Corriea. Franchesca Callejo Guardian Ad Litem for Esmeralda Nolasco.

The Parties addressed/discussed the issue of the utilty bills not being paid by the Corrieas, their positions with regard to the Attorney's fees and the issue of removal of Mr. Lichenstein as Trustee. The Petition for Instructions with regard to the Attorney's fees was continued to September 12, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for written objection to be filed by noon on Friday September 2, 2011. Petition For Instructions Re Eviction Of Beneficiary And Sale Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Apr-25-2011 Continued To Aug-22-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for Esmeralda Nolasco. Paul Epstein for and with Julius Lichtenstein. Gene Sverdlov for and with Maria Corriea.

The Parties reported that Ms. Correira made a payment of $5,000.00, that the Corriea's should now be able to remain current on the rent due to increased income, that the household is now at about $4,000.00 a month, that the Family indicated that $2,000.00 a month could be paid, that the Lender was agreeable to receiving income payments on a regular basis and that the Lender does not wish to foreclose. Mr. Epstein would like to keep the petition on for status of receiving money from the Correia's and will ask for additional funds for past bills. The Parties also spoke of setting up a direct deposit from the Corriea's bank account to the Trust account for payment of the rent. The matter was continued to August 22, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Discussions were held regarding the Corriera's income, the Correira's expenses, arrearages in the utility bills, sale of the property, the possibilty of a life estate, obtaining a report from a Real Estate agent regarding value of the property with/with out repairs and insufficient funds to maintain the property. The matters were continued to April 25, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition Of Beneficiary To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-27-2010 Continued To Mar-28-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo Guardian Ad Litem for Esmeralda Nolasco (Beneficiary). Gene Sverdlov for Maria Corriea (Objector). Paul Stein for and with Julius Lichtensen (Trustee).

Ms. Callejo stated that the Corriea's made two regular payments of $2,000.00, that applications for other income for the Corriea's were still pending, that the Trustee was requesting rent of $2,500.00 and that the lender was willing to wait on the loan. In addition, Ms. Callejo stated that she does not see how Ms. Nolasco could live in the home alone, that property taxes in the amount of $4,600.00 were coming due, that there was back pay on rent, and brought to the Court's attention the issue of the power of attorneys (one for the Daughter and one for the Trustee). Mr. Epstein will provide a further accounting through August 31, 2010 within a week or two. Said accounting will served but not filed. Mr. Sverdlov stated that he was satisfied with what he has received with regard to the accounting at this time, that the Trustee was acting in a more acceptable manner and the he was willing to continue the Petition to Remove Trustee. The Court suggested Counsel talk about the accounting, and resolve when it will be filed and the scope of it. The Petition to Remove Trustee and the Petition for Instructions were continued to March 28, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Instructions Re Eviction Of Beneficiary And Sale Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Aug-09-2010 Continued To Aug-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Evgeni Sverdlov for Maria Corriea (Beneficiary). Paul Epstein and Chalres Triay for Julius Lichtenstein (Trustee). Franchesca Callejo (Guardian Ad Litem) for Esmeralda Nolasco. Nora Lichenstein also present.

Ms. Callejo stated that it was a difficlut situation, that the Parties do not get along, that they do not communicate, that her Client has lived in the property for over 40 years and that she is doing very well for her age. In addition, Ms. Callejo stated that it was the opinion of the Caregiver that Ms. Nolasco remain in the home, that the property has expenses, that she has spoken with both sides, that unless some income is generated or Mr. Lichenstein can extend the loan, that Ms. Nolasco main not be able to remain in the home. Furthermore, Ms. Callejo stated that Ms. Nolasco is being well cared for, that there is no money for the property taxes, that her Client prefers to stay at home and that the proceeds from the loan were used for expenses, taxes and interest payments on the loan. Mr. Epstein stated that he had not discussed extending the loan with the lender. Discussions were held regarding Ms. Nolasco's income and expenses, and Ms. Corriea and her Husband's income, monthly rent and amounts they have paid for repairs/utilities. The Parties are willing to talk and negotiate. Ms. Corriea is to write a check for $2,000.00. Mr. Epstein is to contact Dr. Weingberg (Lender) with regard to flexability on the loan, Mr. and Ms. Corriea are to make efforts to increase their income. The Court will not order an accounting or remove Mr. Lichenstein as Trustee. The matter was continued to August 23, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Various Bailiffs.

Petition For Instructions Re Eviction Of Beneficiary And Sale Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Jun-07-2010 Continued To Jun-28-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gene Sverdlov for Maria Corriea. Paul Epstein for Julius Lichtenstein.

Mr. Sverdlov stated that he was just recently retained. Mr. Lichtenstein stated that there was a question of the competency of Ms. Nolasco, that they would love to keep Ms. Nolasco in the home but that there was no income from the property, that interest-only payments were made every month, that there was no income, that the property taxes were $9,000.00 a year, that the Daughter and the Husband moved into the home, that they do not pay any rent and that there was not enough income to pay off the loan. Mr. Epstein stated that the loan was due August 15, 2010, that the principal balance was $100,000.00 and that the loan was taken out 5 years ago. Mr. Sverdlov stated that Ms. Nolasco does not want to leave the house, that they don't know what the loans are, that they requested paper work but that they did not receive any and that he was told by his Client that the Trustee refused the last rent payment. The matter was continued to June 28, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Sverdlov is to file his objections by close of business June 23, 2010. Mr. Epstein is to provide further information with regard to the loan and file a supplemental declaration as to the same by close of business June 15, 2010. The Court is considering appointing a Guardian Ad Litem for Ms. Nolasco.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
